About us
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) is an event dedicated to advances in computing, artificial intelligence, immersive technologies and digital transformation, bringing together the wide spectrum of fields shaping today’s digital society.
CITDI 2026, organized by the Academic Society for Applied Research, builds upon previous initiatives that have connected researchers and professionals working on intelligent systems, data-driven technologies and emerging digital solutions.
The congress offers a unique opportunity for discussion and collaboration among scientists, engineers, educators, innovators and industry partners, allowing participants to explore applications, share insights and engage with technologies that define the future of digital innovation.

Call for papers
Home /
Call for Papers
Join the Spotlight: Submit to CITDI 2026.
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) invites researchers, academics, industry professionals and practitioners to submit original research contributions addressing advances in digital technologies and intelligent systems.
Submissions may present theoretical developments, applied research, experimental studies or innovative technological applications.
Topics
of Interest
The conference welcomes submissions related, but not limited, to the following thematic areas:
Track 1 — Computing & Smart Cyber-Physical Platforms
➜ Cloud computing, edge and fog computing
➜ Distributed architectures and intelligent infrastructures
➜ Cyber-physical systems and embedded platforms
➜ Internet of Things (IoT) and Industrial IoT (IIoT)
➜ Communication protocols for intelligent ecosystems
➜ Cybersecurity and privacy in connected systems
➜ Virtualization, containers, and automation
➜ Smart grids, energy management, and sustainable systems
➜ Platforms for smart cities, industry, and homes
Track 2 — Artificial Intelligence & Data Science
➜ AI, machine learning, and predictive models
➜ Big data analytics, data mining, and intelligent processing
➜ Modeling, simulation, and cognitive systems
➜ Data-driven decision support systems
➜ Ethical, explainable, and human-centered AI
➜ AI applications in health, transport, industry, and cities
➜ Intelligent automation in complex contexts
Track 3 — Human Interaction & Immersive Tech
➜ Human–computer interaction and user experience
➜ Accessible, inclusive, and adaptive interfaces
➜ Virtual, augmented, and mixed reality
➜ Immersive experiences, serious games, and gamification
➜ Cognition, ergonomics, and human factors
➜ Digital experience design for education, work, and everyday life
➜ Technologies for smart living and digital well-being
Track 4 — Digital Transformation & Innovation
➜ Digital health, telemedicine, and pervasive systems
➜ Technologies for well-being, monitoring, and daily-life support
➜ Digital transformation in organizations and productive sectors
➜ Smart cities, smart mobility, and technological sustainability
➜ Economic, social, and cultural impacts of digital innovation
➜ Data governance, ethics, policies, and technology regulation
➜ Sustainable innovation in industry, education, and public services
➜ Resilient Digital Systems, Security, and Defense Applications

Paper Submission Deadline
Round 1: April 19, 2026

Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original, unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. Submissions must not be simultaneously under review by any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous double-blind peer-review process. Authors must remove names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any identifying information from the submitted PDF.
Types of Contributions
➜ English, following the Springer LNCS format
Papers written in English
➜ Must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Length: 10 to 12 pages (all content included)
➜ Up to 2 extra pages may be purchased if needed
Articles written in Spanish
➜ Must follow the Prismalia Journal author guidelines
➜ Length: 8 to 12 pages (all content included)
➜ Up to 2 extra pages may be purchased if needed
➜ All papers must be submitted in PDF format
➜ The submission portal will be announced on the official conference website
➜ At least one author of each accepted paper must register for the conference to ensure inclusion in the final publication
Submissions will be evaluated based on:
➜ Originality and contribution to the field
➜ Scientific and technical quality
➜ Methodological soundness
➜ Clarity and readability
➜ Relevance to the conference tracks
Only the highest-rated papers will be invited to deliver an oral presentation.
Authors selected for presentation will be assigned:
➜ 10 minutes for oral presentation
➜ 5 minutes for Q&A
Not being selected for presentation does not affect the publication of accepted papers.

Link: Paper Submission
Title: Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
CITDI
Paper Submission
Home /
Paper Submission
Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original and unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. 
Submissions must not be under review or previously published in any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous
double-blind peer-review process
. 
To ensure anonymity, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, 
including names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any references that may reveal the authors’ identity.
Types of Contributions
➜ Full research papers written in English
➜ Manuscripts must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Authors must submit their manuscripts through the official conference submission system. All submissions must be made electronically in PDF format using the conference submission platform.
➜
Submission system
(EasyChair).
➜ Submissions must be original and must not be simultaneously under review in any other conference or journal. Authors are encouraged not to submit more than two papers, including co-authored contributions.
Manuscript Formatting
➜ Formatting guidelines:
Manuscripts must follow the Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) formatting guidelines.
➜ Preparation:
Authors may prepare their manuscripts using Microsoft Word or LaTeX and must convert the final version to PDF before submission.
➜ Template requirement:
Papers must follow the official Springer LNCS template.
➜ Text spacing:
The text must be single-spaced.
➜ Figures and tables:
They should appear within the text close to their first reference.
➜ Fonts:
All fonts must be embedded in the final PDF file.
➜ References:
Citations must follow the LNCS reference format.
➜ Additional information:
Authors may consult the official Springer LNCS author guidelines for detailed formatting rules.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process conducted by members of the Scientific Program Committee.
Each paper will be evaluated according to:
➜ Originality
➜ Technical quality
➜ Methodological rigor
➜ Relevance to the conference topics
➜ Clarity of presentation
Based on the review process, papers may be accepted, accepted with revisions, or rejected.
To ensure a fair review process, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, including:
➜ Author names
➜ Affiliations
➜ Acknowledgments
➜ References revealing authorship
Self-citations should be written in a neutral manner that does not disclose the identity of the authors. Author information will only be included in the camera-ready version after acceptance.
➜ English papers must have a length between 10 and 12 pages, including references, tables, and figures.
➜ Up to two additional pages may be purchased if necessary.
Submitted manuscripts must represent original research contributions. Authors must ensure that the work has not been previously published and is not under consideration elsewhere.
All co-authors must approve the submission. Any form of plagiarism, duplicate submission, or unethical publication practice may result in rejection.
Artificial intelligence tools may be used to assist with writing, editing, or data analysis. However, AI systems cannot be listed as authors. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy, integrity, and originality of the submitted work.
When relevant, the use of AI tools should be transparently disclosed.
Authors are responsible for obtaining permission to reproduce any third-party material included in the manuscript, such as figures, tables, or datasets.
If copyrighted material is used, appropriate permission must be obtained prior to publication and the original source must be properly acknowledged. Please review carefully
here
.
After acceptance, authors must submit the final camera-ready version of their paper following the Springer LNCS formatting requirements.
The final version must include the complete author information and incorporate any revisions requested during the review process.
At least one author must register and present the paper at the conference for the paper to be included in the proceedings.

Registration
Author Registration
Participants and authors must complete the registration process in order to attend CITDI 2026. The registration fee includes access to the scientific sessions of the conference, digital conference materials and participation certificates. Registration does not include travel, accommodation or personal transportation expenses.

students and regular authors.
Other Fees

Author Registration includes:
➜ Participation in all conference sessions
➜ Digital conference documentation
➜ Certificate of participation

➜ Access to keynote sessions and virtual or on-site sessions

Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy
Last updated:
March, 2026
This Privacy Policy explains how
CITDI 2026
(“we”, “us”, “our”) collects, uses, shares, and protects personal data
    when you visit our website, submit a paper, register, attend (virtual or on-site), or interact with our services (the “Services”).
Because this is an international conference, we follow internationally recognized privacy principles and, where applicable, legal frameworks
    such as the
GDPR (EU/UK)
,
CCPA/CPRA
, and similar laws in other jurisdictions.
1) Data Controller / Responsible Entity
➜ Organization: Academic Society for Applied Research
➜ Address: Quito, Ecuador.
➜ Email:
info@asar-research.org
2) Scope
This policy applies to data processed through our conference services, including the website, contact forms, paper submission and peer review,
    registration and payment, virtual platforms, and on-site participation.
3) Personal Data We Collect
➜ Identity & contact data: name, affiliation, email, phone (if provided), country, address (if needed for invoicing).
➜ Submission data: authors, title, abstract, manuscript files, camera-ready files, and review-related information.
➜ Registration data: registration category, attendance mode, invoice details, eligibility information (if applicable).
➜ Payment data: transaction references and payment status (we do not store full card details when handled by processors).
➜ Technical data: IP address, browser/device information, and usage logs (via cookies or similar technologies).
➜ Event media: photos/video/audio recordings during sessions and activities, where announced.
4) Sensitive Data
We do not intentionally request sensitive personal data (e.g., health, biometrics, political opinions). Please avoid including sensitive data in papers.
    If you voluntarily provide it, it will be processed only as necessary for the intended purpose.
5) How We Use Your Data
➜ To manage submissions, peer review, acceptance decisions, and proceedings publication.
➜ To process registrations, provide access to sessions, and issue invoices/receipts.
➜ To communicate important updates (schedule, access links, instructions, policy changes).
➜ To provide support and respond to your questions.
➜ To maintain security, prevent fraud, and protect our website and systems.
➜ To comply with legal obligations (accounting, tax, record-keeping).
6) Legal Bases (International)
➜ Contract: processing needed to deliver registration, participation, and submission services.
➜ Legitimate interests: conference operations, improving services, ensuring security.
➜ Consent: when required (e.g., optional cookies, marketing emails, certain recordings).
➜ Legal obligation: accounting and compliance requirements.
7) Cookies & Analytics
➜ We may use essential cookies for functionality and optional analytics cookies to understand website usage.
➜ You can control cookies through your browser settings and, where available, our cookie preferences tool.
8) Sharing of Data
➜ We may share data with service providers that help us run the conference (submission systems, registration platforms, hosting, email tools).
➜ Payments are processed by third parties (e.g., PayPal, card processors, banks). We do not sell your personal data.
➜ We may disclose data if required by law or to protect rights, safety, and security.
9) International Transfers
Your data may be processed in countries other than your own due to international participation and service providers.
    Where required, we use appropriate safeguards and contractual protections.
10) Data Retention
➜ We keep data only as long as necessary for conference operations and legal obligations.
➜ Proceedings and academic records may be retained longer as part of the scholarly record.
➜ Accounting and invoices are retained according to applicable financial regulations.
11) Security
We apply reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect personal data.
    However, no online service can guarantee absolute security.
12) Your Rights
➜ Depending on your jurisdiction, you may request access, correction, deletion, restriction, objection, or portability of your data.
➜ To request changes or exercise rights, contact:
info@asar-research.org
13) Children’s Privacy
Our services are not directed to children under 16 (or the minimum age required by local law). We do not knowingly collect data from children.
14) Photos / Recordings
The conference may include photos, video, or audio recordings for documentation and dissemination (website, reports, or communication materials),
    where announced. When required, we will provide notice and obtain consent.
15) Third-Party Links
Our website may include links or embedded tools from third parties. We are not responsible for their privacy practices.
    Please review their policies.
16) Changes to This Policy
We may update this policy from time to time. The “Last updated” date will reflect changes.

ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
A
ASAR
Independent Academic Initiative
Promoting Collaboration Between Research, Education & Industry
The Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR) fosters global dialogue and drives innovation in the field of applied research.
Who We Are
A Global Academic Initiative
ASAR is an independent, academic-oriented initiative with global reach. We work to strengthen collaboration between researchers, educators, practitioners, and institutions involved in applied research across different scientific fields.
Our foundation is built on the belief that interdisciplinary cooperation is key to solving complex modern challenges. By bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and industrial application, we create value for society.
Our Mission
"To promote and support applied research by creating opportunities for scientific dissemination, academic dialogue, and cooperation between the academic sector, research institutions, and industry."
Key Objectives
Encourage development and dissemination of applied research.
Facilitate collaboration among researchers and institutions.
Organize conferences and academic meetings.
Support publication in recognized and indexed channels.
Our Pillars
What We Do
Conferences
ASAR promotes international scientific meetings that bring together researchers and professionals. We provide spaces for dialogue, knowledge exchange, and the dissemination of research results.
Publications
We collaborate with journals and editorial partners to support the publication of scientific work derived from our events. We aim to contribute to the visibility of quality research.
Research
Fostering collaboration between academics, universities, and organizations. We promote initiatives that stimulate scientific development, methodological strengthening, and innovation.

Join the Spotlight: Submit to CITDI 2026.
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) invites researchers, academics, industry professionals and practitioners to submit original research contributions addressing advances in digital technologies and intelligent systems.
Submissions may present theoretical developments, applied research, experimental studies or innovative technological applications.
Topics
of Interest
The conference welcomes submissions related, but not limited, to the following thematic areas:
Track 1 — Computing & Smart Cyber-Physical Platforms
➜ Cloud computing, edge and fog computing
➜ Distributed architectures and intelligent infrastructures
➜ Cyber-physical systems and embedded platforms
➜ Internet of Things (IoT) and Industrial IoT (IIoT)
➜ Communication protocols for intelligent ecosystems
➜ Cybersecurity and privacy in connected systems
➜ Virtualization, containers, and automation
➜ Smart grids, energy management, and sustainable systems
➜ Platforms for smart cities, industry, and homes

Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
CITDI
Paper Submission
Home /
Paper Submission
Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original and unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. 
Submissions must not be under review or previously published in any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous
double-blind peer-review process
. 
To ensure anonymity, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, 
including names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any references that may reveal the authors’ identity.
Types of Contributions
➜ Full research papers written in English
➜ Manuscripts must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Authors must submit their manuscripts through the official conference submission system. All submissions must be made electronically in PDF format using the conference submission platform.
➜
Submission system
(EasyChair).
➜ Submissions must be original and must not be simultaneously under review in any other conference or journal. Authors are encouraged not to submit more than two papers, including co-authored contributions.
Manuscript Formatting
➜ Formatting guidelines:
Manuscripts must follow the Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) formatting guidelines.
➜ Preparation:
Authors may prepare their manuscripts using Microsoft Word or LaTeX and must convert the final version to PDF before submission.
➜ Template requirement:
Papers must follow the official Springer LNCS template.
➜ Text spacing:
The text must be single-spaced.
➜ Figures and tables:
They should appear within the text close to their first reference.
➜ Fonts:
All fonts must be embedded in the final PDF file.
➜ References:
Citations must follow the LNCS reference format.
➜ Additional information:
Authors may consult the official Springer LNCS author guidelines for detailed formatting rules.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process conducted by members of the Scientific Program Committee.
Each paper will be evaluated according to:
➜ Originality
➜ Technical quality
➜ Methodological rigor
➜ Relevance to the conference topics
➜ Clarity of presentation
Based on the review process, papers may be accepted, accepted with revisions, or rejected.
To ensure a fair review process, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, including:
➜ Author names
➜ Affiliations
➜ Acknowledgments
➜ References revealing authorship

The Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR) fosters global dialogue and drives innovation in the field of applied research.
Who We Are
A Global Academic Initiative
ASAR is an independent, academic-oriented initiative with global reach. We work to strengthen collaboration between researchers, educators, practitioners, and institutions involved in applied research across different scientific fields.
Our foundation is built on the belief that interdisciplinary cooperation is key to solving complex modern challenges. By bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and industrial application, we create value for society.
Our Mission
"To promote and support applied research by creating opportunities for scientific dissemination, academic dialogue, and cooperation between the academic sector, research institutions, and industry."
Key Objectives
Encourage development and dissemination of applied research.
Facilitate collaboration among researchers and institutions.
Organize conferences and academic meetings.
Support publication in recognized and indexed channels.

Conferences
ASAR promotes international scientific meetings that bring together researchers and professionals. We provide spaces for dialogue, knowledge exchange, and the dissemination of research results.

Privacy Policy
Home /
Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy
Last updated:
March, 2026
This Privacy Policy explains how
CITDI 2026
(“we”, “us”, “our”) collects, uses, shares, and protects personal data
    when you visit our website, submit a paper, register, attend (virtual or on-site), or interact with our services (the “Services”).
Because this is an international conference, we follow internationally recognized privacy principles and, where applicable, legal frameworks
    such as the
GDPR (EU/UK)
,
CCPA/CPRA
, and similar laws in other jurisdictions.
1) Data Controller / Responsible Entity
➜ Organization: Academic Society for Applied Research
➜ Address: Quito, Ecuador.
➜ Email:
info@asar-research.org
2) Scope
This policy applies to data processed through our conference services, including the website, contact forms, paper submission and peer review,
    registration and payment, virtual platforms, and on-site participation.
3) Personal Data We Collect
➜ Identity & contact data: name, affiliation, email, phone (if provided), country, address (if needed for invoicing).
➜ Submission data: authors, title, abstract, manuscript files, camera-ready files, and review-related information.
➜ Registration data: registration category, attendance mode, invoice details, eligibility information (if applicable).
➜ Payment data: transaction references and payment status (we do not store full card details when handled by processors).
➜ Technical data: IP address, browser/device information, and usage logs (via cookies or similar technologies).
➜ Event media: photos/video/audio recordings during sessions and activities, where announced.
4) Sensitive Data
We do not intentionally request sensitive personal data (e.g., health, biometrics, political opinions). Please avoid including sensitive data in papers.
    If you voluntarily provide it, it will be processed only as necessary for the intended purpose.
5) How We Use Your Data
➜ To manage submissions, peer review, acceptance decisions, and proceedings publication.
➜ To process registrations, provide access to sessions, and issue invoices/receipts.
➜ To communicate important updates (schedule, access links, instructions, policy changes).
➜ To provide support and respond to your questions.
➜ To maintain security, prevent fraud, and protect our website and systems.
➜ To comply with legal obligations (accounting, tax, record-keeping).
6) Legal Bases (International)
➜ Contract: processing needed to deliver registration, participation, and submission services.
➜ Legitimate interests: conference operations, improving services, ensuring security.
➜ Consent: when required (e.g., optional cookies, marketing emails, certain recordings).
➜ Legal obligation: accounting and compliance requirements.
7) Cookies & Analytics
➜ We may use essential cookies for functionality and optional analytics cookies to understand website usage.
➜ You can control cookies through your browser settings and, where available, our cookie preferences tool.
8) Sharing of Data
➜ We may share data with service providers that help us run the conference (submission systems, registration platforms, hosting, email tools).
➜ Payments are processed by third parties (e.g., PayPal, card processors, banks). We do not sell your personal data.
➜ We may disclose data if required by law or to protect rights, safety, and security.
9) International Transfers
Your data may be processed in countries other than your own due to international participation and service providers.
    Where required, we use appropriate safeguards and contractual protections.
10) Data Retention
➜ We keep data only as long as necessary for conference operations and legal obligations.
➜ Proceedings and academic records may be retained longer as part of the scholarly record.
➜ Accounting and invoices are retained according to applicable financial regulations.
11) Security
We apply reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect personal data.
    However, no online service can guarantee absolute security.
12) Your Rights
➜ Depending on your jurisdiction, you may request access, correction, deletion, restriction, objection, or portability of your data.
➜ To request changes or exercise rights, contact:
info@asar-research.org
13) Children’s Privacy
Our services are not directed to children under 16 (or the minimum age required by local law). We do not knowingly collect data from children.
14) Photos / Recordings
The conference may include photos, video, or audio recordings for documentation and dissemination (website, reports, or communication materials),
    where announced. When required, we will provide notice and obtain consent.
15) Third-Party Links
Our website may include links or embedded tools from third parties. We are not responsible for their privacy practices.
    Please review their policies.
16) Changes to This Policy
We may update this policy from time to time. The “Last updated” date will reflect changes.

Link: Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR)
Title: ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
A
ASAR
Independent Academic Initiative
Promoting Collaboration Between Research, Education & Industry
The Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR) fosters global dialogue and drives innovation in the field of applied research.
Who We Are
A Global Academic Initiative
ASAR is an independent, academic-oriented initiative with global reach. We work to strengthen collaboration between researchers, educators, practitioners, and institutions involved in applied research across different scientific fields.
Our foundation is built on the belief that interdisciplinary cooperation is key to solving complex modern challenges. By bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and industrial application, we create value for society.
Our Mission
"To promote and support applied research by creating opportunities for scientific dissemination, academic dialogue, and cooperation between the academic sector, research institutions, and industry."
Key Objectives
Encourage development and dissemination of applied research.
Facilitate collaboration among researchers and institutions.
Organize conferences and academic meetings.
Support publication in recognized and indexed channels.
Our Pillars
What We Do
Conferences
ASAR promotes international scientific meetings that bring together researchers and professionals. We provide spaces for dialogue, knowledge exchange, and the dissemination of research results.
Publications
We collaborate with journals and editorial partners to support the publication of scientific work derived from our events. We aim to contribute to the visibility of quality research.
Research
Fostering collaboration between academics, universities, and organizations. We promote initiatives that stimulate scientific development, methodological strengthening, and innovation.

About us
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) is an event dedicated to advances in computing, artificial intelligence, immersive technologies and digital transformation, bringing together the wide spectrum of fields shaping today’s digital society.
CITDI 2026, organized by the Academic Society for Applied Research, builds upon previous initiatives that have connected researchers and professionals working on intelligent systems, data-driven technologies and emerging digital solutions.
The congress offers a unique opportunity for discussion and collaboration among scientists, engineers, educators, innovators and industry partners, allowing participants to explore applications, share insights and engage with technologies that define the future of digital innovation.

Title: Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
CITDI
Paper Submission
Home /
Paper Submission
Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original and unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. 
Submissions must not be under review or previously published in any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous
double-blind peer-review process
. 
To ensure anonymity, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, 
including names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any references that may reveal the authors’ identity.
Types of Contributions
➜ Full research papers written in English
➜ Manuscripts must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Authors must submit their manuscripts through the official conference submission system. All submissions must be made electronically in PDF format using the conference submission platform.
➜
Submission system
(EasyChair).
➜ Submissions must be original and must not be simultaneously under review in any other conference or journal. Authors are encouraged not to submit more than two papers, including co-authored contributions.
Manuscript Formatting
➜ Formatting guidelines:
Manuscripts must follow the Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) formatting guidelines.
➜ Preparation:
Authors may prepare their manuscripts using Microsoft Word or LaTeX and must convert the final version to PDF before submission.
➜ Template requirement:
Papers must follow the official Springer LNCS template.
➜ Text spacing:
The text must be single-spaced.
➜ Figures and tables:
They should appear within the text close to their first reference.
➜ Fonts:
All fonts must be embedded in the final PDF file.
➜ References:
Citations must follow the LNCS reference format.
➜ Additional information:
Authors may consult the official Springer LNCS author guidelines for detailed formatting rules.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process conducted by members of the Scientific Program Committee.
Each paper will be evaluated according to:
➜ Originality
➜ Technical quality
➜ Methodological rigor
➜ Relevance to the conference topics
➜ Clarity of presentation
Based on the review process, papers may be accepted, accepted with revisions, or rejected.
To ensure a fair review process, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, including:
➜ Author names
➜ Affiliations
➜ Acknowledgments
➜ References revealing authorship
Self-citations should be written in a neutral manner that does not disclose the identity of the authors. Author information will only be included in the camera-ready version after acceptance.
➜ English papers must have a length between 10 and 12 pages, including references, tables, and figures.
➜ Up to two additional pages may be purchased if necessary.
Submitted manuscripts must represent original research contributions. Authors must ensure that the work has not been previously published and is not under consideration elsewhere.
All co-authors must approve the submission. Any form of plagiarism, duplicate submission, or unethical publication practice may result in rejection.
Artificial intelligence tools may be used to assist with writing, editing, or data analysis. However, AI systems cannot be listed as authors. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy, integrity, and originality of the submitted work.
When relevant, the use of AI tools should be transparently disclosed.
Authors are responsible for obtaining permission to reproduce any third-party material included in the manuscript, such as figures, tables, or datasets.
If copyrighted material is used, appropriate permission must be obtained prior to publication and the original source must be properly acknowledged. Please review carefully
here
.
After acceptance, authors must submit the final camera-ready version of their paper following the Springer LNCS formatting requirements.
The final version must include the complete author information and incorporate any revisions requested during the review process.
At least one author must register and present the paper at the conference for the paper to be included in the proceedings.

Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy
Last updated:
March, 2026
This Privacy Policy explains how
CITDI 2026
(“we”, “us”, “our”) collects, uses, shares, and protects personal data
    when you visit our website, submit a paper, register, attend (virtual or on-site), or interact with our services (the “Services”).
Because this is an international conference, we follow internationally recognized privacy principles and, where applicable, legal frameworks
    such as the
GDPR (EU/UK)
,
CCPA/CPRA
, and similar laws in other jurisdictions.
1) Data Controller / Responsible Entity
➜ Organization: Academic Society for Applied Research
➜ Address: Quito, Ecuador.
➜ Email:
info@asar-research.org
2) Scope
This policy applies to data processed through our conference services, including the website, contact forms, paper submission and peer review,
    registration and payment, virtual platforms, and on-site participation.
3) Personal Data We Collect
➜ Identity & contact data: name, affiliation, email, phone (if provided), country, address (if needed for invoicing).
➜ Submission data: authors, title, abstract, manuscript files, camera-ready files, and review-related information.
➜ Registration data: registration category, attendance mode, invoice details, eligibility information (if applicable).
➜ Payment data: transaction references and payment status (we do not store full card details when handled by processors).
➜ Technical data: IP address, browser/device information, and usage logs (via cookies or similar technologies).
➜ Event media: photos/video/audio recordings during sessions and activities, where announced.
4) Sensitive Data
We do not intentionally request sensitive personal data (e.g., health, biometrics, political opinions). Please avoid including sensitive data in papers.
    If you voluntarily provide it, it will be processed only as necessary for the intended purpose.
5) How We Use Your Data
➜ To manage submissions, peer review, acceptance decisions, and proceedings publication.
➜ To process registrations, provide access to sessions, and issue invoices/receipts.
➜ To communicate important updates (schedule, access links, instructions, policy changes).
➜ To provide support and respond to your questions.
➜ To maintain security, prevent fraud, and protect our website and systems.
➜ To comply with legal obligations (accounting, tax, record-keeping).
6) Legal Bases (International)
➜ Contract: processing needed to deliver registration, participation, and submission services.
➜ Legitimate interests: conference operations, improving services, ensuring security.
➜ Consent: when required (e.g., optional cookies, marketing emails, certain recordings).
➜ Legal obligation: accounting and compliance requirements.
7) Cookies & Analytics
➜ We may use essential cookies for functionality and optional analytics cookies to understand website usage.
➜ You can control cookies through your browser settings and, where available, our cookie preferences tool.
8) Sharing of Data
➜ We may share data with service providers that help us run the conference (submission systems, registration platforms, hosting, email tools).
➜ Payments are processed by third parties (e.g., PayPal, card processors, banks). We do not sell your personal data.
➜ We may disclose data if required by law or to protect rights, safety, and security.
9) International Transfers
Your data may be processed in countries other than your own due to international participation and service providers.
    Where required, we use appropriate safeguards and contractual protections.
10) Data Retention
➜ We keep data only as long as necessary for conference operations and legal obligations.
➜ Proceedings and academic records may be retained longer as part of the scholarly record.

ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
A
ASAR
Independent Academic Initiative
Promoting Collaboration Between Research, Education & Industry
The Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR) fosters global dialogue and drives innovation in the field of applied research.
Who We Are
A Global Academic Initiative
ASAR is an independent, academic-oriented initiative with global reach. We work to strengthen collaboration between researchers, educators, practitioners, and institutions involved in applied research across different scientific fields.
Our foundation is built on the belief that interdisciplinary cooperation is key to solving complex modern challenges. By bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and industrial application, we create value for society.
Our Mission
"To promote and support applied research by creating opportunities for scientific dissemination, academic dialogue, and cooperation between the academic sector, research institutions, and industry."
Key Objectives
Encourage development and dissemination of applied research.
Facilitate collaboration among researchers and institutions.
Organize conferences and academic meetings.
Support publication in recognized and indexed channels.
Our Pillars
What We Do
Conferences
ASAR promotes international scientific meetings that bring together researchers and professionals. We provide spaces for dialogue, knowledge exchange, and the dissemination of research results.
Publications
We collaborate with journals and editorial partners to support the publication of scientific work derived from our events. We aim to contribute to the visibility of quality research.
Research
Fostering collaboration between academics, universities, and organizations. We promote initiatives that stimulate scientific development, methodological strengthening, and innovation.

Join the Spotlight: Submit to CITDI 2026.
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) invites researchers, academics, industry professionals and practitioners to submit original research contributions addressing advances in digital technologies and intelligent systems.
Submissions may present theoretical developments, applied research, experimental studies or innovative technological applications.

Track 4 — Digital Transformation & Innovation

Link: Paper Submission
Title: Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
CITDI
Paper Submission
Home /
Paper Submission
Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original and unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. 
Submissions must not be under review or previously published in any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous
double-blind peer-review process
. 
To ensure anonymity, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, 
including names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any references that may reveal the authors’ identity.
Types of Contributions
➜ Full research papers written in English
➜ Manuscripts must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Authors must submit their manuscripts through the official conference submission system. All submissions must be made electronically in PDF format using the conference submission platform.
➜
Submission system
(EasyChair).
➜ Submissions must be original and must not be simultaneously under review in any other conference or journal. Authors are encouraged not to submit more than two papers, including co-authored contributions.
Manuscript Formatting
➜ Formatting guidelines:
Manuscripts must follow the Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) formatting guidelines.
➜ Preparation:
Authors may prepare their manuscripts using Microsoft Word or LaTeX and must convert the final version to PDF before submission.
➜ Template requirement:
Papers must follow the official Springer LNCS template.
➜ Text spacing:
The text must be single-spaced.
➜ Figures and tables:
They should appear within the text close to their first reference.
➜ Fonts:
All fonts must be embedded in the final PDF file.
➜ References:
Citations must follow the LNCS reference format.
➜ Additional information:
Authors may consult the official Springer LNCS author guidelines for detailed formatting rules.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process conducted by members of the Scientific Program Committee.
Each paper will be evaluated according to:
➜ Originality
➜ Technical quality
➜ Methodological rigor
➜ Relevance to the conference topics
➜ Clarity of presentation
Based on the review process, papers may be accepted, accepted with revisions, or rejected.
To ensure a fair review process, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, including:
➜ Author names
➜ Affiliations
➜ Acknowledgments
➜ References revealing authorship
Self-citations should be written in a neutral manner that does not disclose the identity of the authors. Author information will only be included in the camera-ready version after acceptance.
➜ English papers must have a length between 10 and 12 pages, including references, tables, and figures.
➜ Up to two additional pages may be purchased if necessary.
Submitted manuscripts must represent original research contributions. Authors must ensure that the work has not been previously published and is not under consideration elsewhere.
All co-authors must approve the submission. Any form of plagiarism, duplicate submission, or unethical publication practice may result in rejection.
Artificial intelligence tools may be used to assist with writing, editing, or data analysis. However, AI systems cannot be listed as authors. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy, integrity, and originality of the submitted work.
When relevant, the use of AI tools should be transparently disclosed.
Authors are responsible for obtaining permission to reproduce any third-party material included in the manuscript, such as figures, tables, or datasets.
If copyrighted material is used, appropriate permission must be obtained prior to publication and the original source must be properly acknowledged. Please review carefully
here
.
After acceptance, authors must submit the final camera-ready version of their paper following the Springer LNCS formatting requirements.
The final version must include the complete author information and incorporate any revisions requested during the review process.
At least one author must register and present the paper at the conference for the paper to be included in the proceedings.

Title: Privacy Policy – CITDI
Privacy Policy – CITDI
CITDI
Privacy Policy
Home /
Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy
Last updated:
March, 2026
This Privacy Policy explains how
CITDI 2026
(“we”, “us”, “our”) collects, uses, shares, and protects personal data
    when you visit our website, submit a paper, register, attend (virtual or on-site), or interact with our services (the “Services”).
Because this is an international conference, we follow internationally recognized privacy principles and, where applicable, legal frameworks
    such as the
GDPR (EU/UK)
,
CCPA/CPRA
, and similar laws in other jurisdictions.
1) Data Controller / Responsible Entity
➜ Organization: Academic Society for Applied Research
➜ Address: Quito, Ecuador.
➜ Email:
info@asar-research.org
2) Scope
This policy applies to data processed through our conference services, including the website, contact forms, paper submission and peer review,
    registration and payment, virtual platforms, and on-site participation.
3) Personal Data We Collect
➜ Identity & contact data: name, affiliation, email, phone (if provided), country, address (if needed for invoicing).
➜ Submission data: authors, title, abstract, manuscript files, camera-ready files, and review-related information.
➜ Registration data: registration category, attendance mode, invoice details, eligibility information (if applicable).
➜ Payment data: transaction references and payment status (we do not store full card details when handled by processors).
➜ Technical data: IP address, browser/device information, and usage logs (via cookies or similar technologies).
➜ Event media: photos/video/audio recordings during sessions and activities, where announced.
4) Sensitive Data
We do not intentionally request sensitive personal data (e.g., health, biometrics, political opinions). Please avoid including sensitive data in papers.
    If you voluntarily provide it, it will be processed only as necessary for the intended purpose.
5) How We Use Your Data
➜ To manage submissions, peer review, acceptance decisions, and proceedings publication.
➜ To process registrations, provide access to sessions, and issue invoices/receipts.
➜ To communicate important updates (schedule, access links, instructions, policy changes).
➜ To provide support and respond to your questions.
➜ To maintain security, prevent fraud, and protect our website and systems.
➜ To comply with legal obligations (accounting, tax, record-keeping).
6) Legal Bases (International)
➜ Contract: processing needed to deliver registration, participation, and submission services.
➜ Legitimate interests: conference operations, improving services, ensuring security.
➜ Consent: when required (e.g., optional cookies, marketing emails, certain recordings).
➜ Legal obligation: accounting and compliance requirements.
7) Cookies & Analytics
➜ We may use essential cookies for functionality and optional analytics cookies to understand website usage.
➜ You can control cookies through your browser settings and, where available, our cookie preferences tool.
8) Sharing of Data
➜ We may share data with service providers that help us run the conference (submission systems, registration platforms, hosting, email tools).
➜ Payments are processed by third parties (e.g., PayPal, card processors, banks). We do not sell your personal data.
➜ We may disclose data if required by law or to protect rights, safety, and security.
9) International Transfers
Your data may be processed in countries other than your own due to international participation and service providers.
    Where required, we use appropriate safeguards and contractual protections.
10) Data Retention
➜ We keep data only as long as necessary for conference operations and legal obligations.
➜ Proceedings and academic records may be retained longer as part of the scholarly record.
➜ Accounting and invoices are retained according to applicable financial regulations.
11) Security
We apply reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect personal data.
    However, no online service can guarantee absolute security.
12) Your Rights
➜ Depending on your jurisdiction, you may request access, correction, deletion, restriction, objection, or portability of your data.
➜ To request changes or exercise rights, contact:
info@asar-research.org
13) Children’s Privacy
Our services are not directed to children under 16 (or the minimum age required by local law). We do not knowingly collect data from children.
14) Photos / Recordings
The conference may include photos, video, or audio recordings for documentation and dissemination (website, reports, or communication materials),
    where announced. When required, we will provide notice and obtain consent.
15) Third-Party Links
Our website may include links or embedded tools from third parties. We are not responsible for their privacy practices.
    Please review their policies.
16) Changes to This Policy
We may update this policy from time to time. The “Last updated” date will reflect changes.

Link: Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR)
Title: ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
A
ASAR
Independent Academic Initiative
Promoting Collaboration Between Research, Education & Industry
The Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR) fosters global dialogue and drives innovation in the field of applied research.
Who We Are
A Global Academic Initiative
ASAR is an independent, academic-oriented initiative with global reach. We work to strengthen collaboration between researchers, educators, practitioners, and institutions involved in applied research across different scientific fields.
Our foundation is built on the belief that interdisciplinary cooperation is key to solving complex modern challenges. By bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and industrial application, we create value for society.
Our Mission
"To promote and support applied research by creating opportunities for scientific dissemination, academic dialogue, and cooperation between the academic sector, research institutions, and industry."
Key Objectives
Encourage development and dissemination of applied research.
Facilitate collaboration among researchers and institutions.
Organize conferences and academic meetings.
Support publication in recognized and indexed channels.
Our Pillars
What We Do
Conferences
ASAR promotes international scientific meetings that bring together researchers and professionals. We provide spaces for dialogue, knowledge exchange, and the dissemination of research results.
Publications
We collaborate with journals and editorial partners to support the publication of scientific work derived from our events. We aim to contribute to the visibility of quality research.
Research
Fostering collaboration between academics, universities, and organizations. We promote initiatives that stimulate scientific development, methodological strengthening, and innovation.

Call for Papers — CITDI 2026
CITDI
Call for papers
Home /
Call for Papers
Join the Spotlight: Submit to CITDI 2026.
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) invites researchers, academics, industry professionals and practitioners to submit original research contributions addressing advances in digital technologies and intelligent systems.
Submissions may present theoretical developments, applied research, experimental studies or innovative technological applications.
Topics
of Interest
The conference welcomes submissions related, but not limited, to the following thematic areas:
Track 1 — Computing & Smart Cyber-Physical Platforms
➜ Cloud computing, edge and fog computing
➜ Distributed architectures and intelligent infrastructures
➜ Cyber-physical systems and embedded platforms
➜ Internet of Things (IoT) and Industrial IoT (IIoT)
➜ Communication protocols for intelligent ecosystems
➜ Cybersecurity and privacy in connected systems
➜ Virtualization, containers, and automation
➜ Smart grids, energy management, and sustainable systems
➜ Platforms for smart cities, industry, and homes
Track 2 — Artificial Intelligence & Data Science
➜ AI, machine learning, and predictive models
➜ Big data analytics, data mining, and intelligent processing
➜ Modeling, simulation, and cognitive systems
➜ Data-driven decision support systems
➜ Ethical, explainable, and human-centered AI
➜ AI applications in health, transport, industry, and cities
➜ Intelligent automation in complex contexts
Track 3 — Human Interaction & Immersive Tech
➜ Human–computer interaction and user experience
➜ Accessible, inclusive, and adaptive interfaces
➜ Virtual, augmented, and mixed reality
➜ Immersive experiences, serious games, and gamification
➜ Cognition, ergonomics, and human factors
➜ Digital experience design for education, work, and everyday life
➜ Technologies for smart living and digital well-being
Track 4 — Digital Transformation & Innovation
➜ Digital health, telemedicine, and pervasive systems
➜ Technologies for well-being, monitoring, and daily-life support
➜ Digital transformation in organizations and productive sectors
➜ Smart cities, smart mobility, and technological sustainability
➜ Economic, social, and cultural impacts of digital innovation
➜ Data governance, ethics, policies, and technology regulation
➜ Sustainable innovation in industry, education, and public services
➜ Resilient Digital Systems, Security, and Defense Applications
Important Dates
Paper Submission Deadline
Round 1: April 19, 2026
Round 2: May 31, 2026
Notification of Acceptance
July 12, 2026
Camera-ready & Registration
July 26, 2026
Conference Dates
August 26-28, 2026
Looking to Join CITDI 2026?
Register
Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original, unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. Submissions must not be simultaneously under review by any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous double-blind peer-review process. Authors must remove names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any identifying information from the submitted PDF.
Types of Contributions
➜ English, following the Springer LNCS format
Papers written in English
➜ Must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Length: 10 to 12 pages (all content included)
➜ Up to 2 extra pages may be purchased if needed
Articles written in Spanish
➜ Must follow the Prismalia Journal author guidelines
➜ Length: 8 to 12 pages (all content included)
➜ Up to 2 extra pages may be purchased if needed
➜ All papers must be submitted in PDF format
➜ The submission portal will be announced on the official conference website
➜ At least one author of each accepted paper must register for the conference to ensure inclusion in the final publication
Submissions will be evaluated based on:
➜ Originality and contribution to the field
➜ Scientific and technical quality
➜ Methodological soundness
➜ Clarity and readability
➜ Relevance to the conference tracks
Only the highest-rated papers will be invited to deliver an oral presentation.
Authors selected for presentation will be assigned:
➜ 10 minutes for oral presentation
➜ 5 minutes for Q&A
Not being selected for presentation does not affect the publication of accepted papers.

Link: Paper Submission
Title: Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
CITDI
Paper Submission
Home /
Paper Submission
Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original and unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. 
Submissions must not be under review or previously published in any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous
double-blind peer-review process
. 
To ensure anonymity, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, 
including names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any references that may reveal the authors’ identity.
Types of Contributions
➜ Full research papers written in English
➜ Manuscripts must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Authors must submit their manuscripts through the official conference submission system. All submissions must be made electronically in PDF format using the conference submission platform.
➜
Submission system
(EasyChair).
➜ Submissions must be original and must not be simultaneously under review in any other conference or journal. Authors are encouraged not to submit more than two papers, including co-authored contributions.
Manuscript Formatting
➜ Formatting guidelines:
Manuscripts must follow the Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) formatting guidelines.
➜ Preparation:
Authors may prepare their manuscripts using Microsoft Word or LaTeX and must convert the final version to PDF before submission.
➜ Template requirement:
Papers must follow the official Springer LNCS template.
➜ Text spacing:
The text must be single-spaced.
➜ Figures and tables:
They should appear within the text close to their first reference.
➜ Fonts:
All fonts must be embedded in the final PDF file.
➜ References:
Citations must follow the LNCS reference format.
➜ Additional information:
Authors may consult the official Springer LNCS author guidelines for detailed formatting rules.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process conducted by members of the Scientific Program Committee.
Each paper will be evaluated according to:
➜ Originality
➜ Technical quality
➜ Methodological rigor
➜ Relevance to the conference topics
➜ Clarity of presentation
Based on the review process, papers may be accepted, accepted with revisions, or rejected.
To ensure a fair review process, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, including:
➜ Author names
➜ Affiliations
➜ Acknowledgments
➜ References revealing authorship
Self-citations should be written in a neutral manner that does not disclose the identity of the authors. Author information will only be included in the camera-ready version after acceptance.
➜ English papers must have a length between 10 and 12 pages, including references, tables, and figures.
➜ Up to two additional pages may be purchased if necessary.
Submitted manuscripts must represent original research contributions. Authors must ensure that the work has not been previously published and is not under consideration elsewhere.
All co-authors must approve the submission. Any form of plagiarism, duplicate submission, or unethical publication practice may result in rejection.
Artificial intelligence tools may be used to assist with writing, editing, or data analysis. However, AI systems cannot be listed as authors. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy, integrity, and originality of the submitted work.
When relevant, the use of AI tools should be transparently disclosed.
Authors are responsible for obtaining permission to reproduce any third-party material included in the manuscript, such as figures, tables, or datasets.
If copyrighted material is used, appropriate permission must be obtained prior to publication and the original source must be properly acknowledged. Please review carefully
here
.
After acceptance, authors must submit the final camera-ready version of their paper following the Springer LNCS formatting requirements.
The final version must include the complete author information and incorporate any revisions requested during the review process.
At least one author must register and present the paper at the conference for the paper to be included in the proceedings.

This Privacy Policy explains how
CITDI 2026
(“we”, “us”, “our”) collects, uses, shares, and protects personal data
    when you visit our website, submit a paper, register, attend (virtual or on-site), or interact with our services (the “Services”).
Because this is an international conference, we follow internationally recognized privacy principles and, where applicable, legal frameworks
    such as the
GDPR (EU/UK)
,
CCPA/CPRA
, and similar laws in other jurisdictions.
1) Data Controller / Responsible Entity
➜ Organization: Academic Society for Applied Research
➜ Address: Quito, Ecuador.

info@asar-research.org
2) Scope
This policy applies to data processed through our conference services, including the website, contact forms, paper submission and peer review,
    registration and payment, virtual platforms, and on-site participation.
3) Personal Data We Collect
➜ Identity & contact data: name, affiliation, email, phone (if provided), country, address (if needed for invoicing).
➜ Submission data: authors, title, abstract, manuscript files, camera-ready files, and review-related information.
➜ Registration data: registration category, attendance mode, invoice details, eligibility information (if applicable).
➜ Payment data: transaction references and payment status (we do not store full card details when handled by processors).
➜ Technical data: IP address, browser/device information, and usage logs (via cookies or similar technologies).
➜ Event media: photos/video/audio recordings during sessions and activities, where announced.
4) Sensitive Data
We do not intentionally request sensitive personal data (e.g., health, biometrics, political opinions). Please avoid including sensitive data in papers.
    If you voluntarily provide it, it will be processed only as necessary for the intended purpose.
5) How We Use Your Data
➜ To manage submissions, peer review, acceptance decisions, and proceedings publication.
➜ To process registrations, provide access to sessions, and issue invoices/receipts.
➜ To communicate important updates (schedule, access links, instructions, policy changes).
➜ To provide support and respond to your questions.
➜ To maintain security, prevent fraud, and protect our website and systems.
➜ To comply with legal obligations (accounting, tax, record-keeping).
6) Legal Bases (International)
➜ Contract: processing needed to deliver registration, participation, and submission services.
➜ Legitimate interests: conference operations, improving services, ensuring security.
➜ Consent: when required (e.g., optional cookies, marketing emails, certain recordings).
➜ Legal obligation: accounting and compliance requirements.
7) Cookies & Analytics
➜ We may use essential cookies for functionality and optional analytics cookies to understand website usage.
➜ You can control cookies through your browser settings and, where available, our cookie preferences tool.
8) Sharing of Data
➜ We may share data with service providers that help us run the conference (submission systems, registration platforms, hosting, email tools).
➜ Payments are processed by third parties (e.g., PayPal, card processors, banks). We do not sell your personal data.
➜ We may disclose data if required by law or to protect rights, safety, and security.
9) International Transfers
Your data may be processed in countries other than your own due to international participation and service providers.
    Where required, we use appropriate safeguards and contractual protections.
10) Data Retention
➜ We keep data only as long as necessary for conference operations and legal obligations.
➜ Proceedings and academic records may be retained longer as part of the scholarly record.
➜ Accounting and invoices are retained according to applicable financial regulations.
11) Security
We apply reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect personal data.
    However, no online service can guarantee absolute security.
12) Your Rights
➜ Depending on your jurisdiction, you may request access, correction, deletion, restriction, objection, or portability of your data.
➜ To request changes or exercise rights, contact:
info@asar-research.org
13) Children’s Privacy
Our services are not directed to children under 16 (or the minimum age required by local law). We do not knowingly collect data from children.
14) Photos / Recordings
The conference may include photos, video, or audio recordings for documentation and dissemination (website, reports, or communication materials),
    where announced. When required, we will provide notice and obtain consent.
15) Third-Party Links
Our website may include links or embedded tools from third parties. We are not responsible for their privacy practices.
    Please review their policies.

ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
A
ASAR
Independent Academic Initiative
Promoting Collaboration Between Research, Education & Industry
The Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR) fosters global dialogue and drives innovation in the field of applied research.
Who We Are
A Global Academic Initiative
ASAR is an independent, academic-oriented initiative with global reach. We work to strengthen collaboration between researchers, educators, practitioners, and institutions involved in applied research across different scientific fields.
Our foundation is built on the belief that interdisciplinary cooperation is key to solving complex modern challenges. By bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and industrial application, we create value for society.
Our Mission
"To promote and support applied research by creating opportunities for scientific dissemination, academic dialogue, and cooperation between the academic sector, research institutions, and industry."
Key Objectives
Encourage development and dissemination of applied research.
Facilitate collaboration among researchers and institutions.
Organize conferences and academic meetings.
Support publication in recognized and indexed channels.
Our Pillars
What We Do
Conferences
ASAR promotes international scientific meetings that bring together researchers and professionals. We provide spaces for dialogue, knowledge exchange, and the dissemination of research results.
Publications
We collaborate with journals and editorial partners to support the publication of scientific work derived from our events. We aim to contribute to the visibility of quality research.
Research
Fostering collaboration between academics, universities, and organizations. We promote initiatives that stimulate scientific development, methodological strengthening, and innovation.

About us
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) is an event dedicated to advances in computing, artificial intelligence, immersive technologies and digital transformation, bringing together the wide spectrum of fields shaping today’s digital society.
CITDI 2026, organized by the Academic Society for Applied Research, builds upon previous initiatives that have connected researchers and professionals working on intelligent systems, data-driven technologies and emerging digital solutions.
The congress offers a unique opportunity for discussion and collaboration among scientists, engineers, educators, innovators and industry partners, allowing participants to explore applications, share insights and engage with technologies that define the future of digital innovation.

Join the Spotlight: Submit to CITDI 2026.
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) invites researchers, academics, industry professionals and practitioners to submit original research contributions addressing advances in digital technologies and intelligent systems.
Submissions may present theoretical developments, applied research, experimental studies or innovative technological applications.

of Interest

Track 1 — Computing & Smart Cyber-Physical Platforms

Link: Paper Submission
Title: Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
Paper Submission Guidelines — CITDI 2026
CITDI
Paper Submission
Home /
Paper Submission
Paper Submission
Authors are invited to submit original and unpublished research contributions for presentation at CITDI 2026. 
Submissions must not be under review or previously published in any other conference or journal.
All manuscripts will undergo a rigorous
double-blind peer-review process
. 
To ensure anonymity, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, 
including names, affiliations, acknowledgments, and any references that may reveal the authors’ identity.
Types of Contributions
➜ Full research papers written in English
➜ Manuscripts must follow the Springer LNCS formatting guidelines
➜ Authors must submit their manuscripts through the official conference submission system. All submissions must be made electronically in PDF format using the conference submission platform.
➜
Submission system
(EasyChair).
➜ Submissions must be original and must not be simultaneously under review in any other conference or journal. Authors are encouraged not to submit more than two papers, including co-authored contributions.
Manuscript Formatting
➜ Formatting guidelines:
Manuscripts must follow the Springer Lecture Notes in Computer Science (LNCS) formatting guidelines.
➜ Preparation:
Authors may prepare their manuscripts using Microsoft Word or LaTeX and must convert the final version to PDF before submission.
➜ Template requirement:
Papers must follow the official Springer LNCS template.
➜ Text spacing:
The text must be single-spaced.
➜ Figures and tables:
They should appear within the text close to their first reference.
➜ Fonts:
All fonts must be embedded in the final PDF file.
➜ References:
Citations must follow the LNCS reference format.
➜ Additional information:
Authors may consult the official Springer LNCS author guidelines for detailed formatting rules.
All submissions will undergo a double-blind peer review process conducted by members of the Scientific Program Committee.
Each paper will be evaluated according to:
➜ Originality
➜ Technical quality
➜ Methodological rigor
➜ Relevance to the conference topics
➜ Clarity of presentation
Based on the review process, papers may be accepted, accepted with revisions, or rejected.
To ensure a fair review process, authors must remove all identifying information from the submitted manuscript, including:
➜ Author names
➜ Affiliations
➜ Acknowledgments
➜ References revealing authorship
Self-citations should be written in a neutral manner that does not disclose the identity of the authors. Author information will only be included in the camera-ready version after acceptance.
➜ English papers must have a length between 10 and 12 pages, including references, tables, and figures.
➜ Up to two additional pages may be purchased if necessary.
Submitted manuscripts must represent original research contributions. Authors must ensure that the work has not been previously published and is not under consideration elsewhere.
All co-authors must approve the submission. Any form of plagiarism, duplicate submission, or unethical publication practice may result in rejection.
Artificial intelligence tools may be used to assist with writing, editing, or data analysis. However, AI systems cannot be listed as authors. Authors remain fully responsible for the accuracy, integrity, and originality of the submitted work.
When relevant, the use of AI tools should be transparently disclosed.
Authors are responsible for obtaining permission to reproduce any third-party material included in the manuscript, such as figures, tables, or datasets.
If copyrighted material is used, appropriate permission must be obtained prior to publication and the original source must be properly acknowledged. Please review carefully
here
.
After acceptance, authors must submit the final camera-ready version of their paper following the Springer LNCS formatting requirements.
The final version must include the complete author information and incorporate any revisions requested during the review process.
At least one author must register and present the paper at the conference for the paper to be included in the proceedings.

Privacy Policy
Home /
Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy
Last updated:
March, 2026
This Privacy Policy explains how
CITDI 2026
(“we”, “us”, “our”) collects, uses, shares, and protects personal data
    when you visit our website, submit a paper, register, attend (virtual or on-site), or interact with our services (the “Services”).
Because this is an international conference, we follow internationally recognized privacy principles and, where applicable, legal frameworks
    such as the
GDPR (EU/UK)
,
CCPA/CPRA
, and similar laws in other jurisdictions.
1) Data Controller / Responsible Entity
➜ Organization: Academic Society for Applied Research
➜ Address: Quito, Ecuador.
➜ Email:
info@asar-research.org
2) Scope
This policy applies to data processed through our conference services, including the website, contact forms, paper submission and peer review,
    registration and payment, virtual platforms, and on-site participation.
3) Personal Data We Collect
➜ Identity & contact data: name, affiliation, email, phone (if provided), country, address (if needed for invoicing).
➜ Submission data: authors, title, abstract, manuscript files, camera-ready files, and review-related information.
➜ Registration data: registration category, attendance mode, invoice details, eligibility information (if applicable).
➜ Payment data: transaction references and payment status (we do not store full card details when handled by processors).
➜ Technical data: IP address, browser/device information, and usage logs (via cookies or similar technologies).
➜ Event media: photos/video/audio recordings during sessions and activities, where announced.
4) Sensitive Data
We do not intentionally request sensitive personal data (e.g., health, biometrics, political opinions). Please avoid including sensitive data in papers.
    If you voluntarily provide it, it will be processed only as necessary for the intended purpose.
5) How We Use Your Data
➜ To manage submissions, peer review, acceptance decisions, and proceedings publication.
➜ To process registrations, provide access to sessions, and issue invoices/receipts.
➜ To communicate important updates (schedule, access links, instructions, policy changes).
➜ To provide support and respond to your questions.
➜ To maintain security, prevent fraud, and protect our website and systems.
➜ To comply with legal obligations (accounting, tax, record-keeping).
6) Legal Bases (International)
➜ Contract: processing needed to deliver registration, participation, and submission services.
➜ Legitimate interests: conference operations, improving services, ensuring security.
➜ Consent: when required (e.g., optional cookies, marketing emails, certain recordings).
➜ Legal obligation: accounting and compliance requirements.
7) Cookies & Analytics
➜ We may use essential cookies for functionality and optional analytics cookies to understand website usage.
➜ You can control cookies through your browser settings and, where available, our cookie preferences tool.
8) Sharing of Data
➜ We may share data with service providers that help us run the conference (submission systems, registration platforms, hosting, email tools).
➜ Payments are processed by third parties (e.g., PayPal, card processors, banks). We do not sell your personal data.
➜ We may disclose data if required by law or to protect rights, safety, and security.
9) International Transfers
Your data may be processed in countries other than your own due to international participation and service providers.
    Where required, we use appropriate safeguards and contractual protections.
10) Data Retention
➜ We keep data only as long as necessary for conference operations and legal obligations.
➜ Proceedings and academic records may be retained longer as part of the scholarly record.
➜ Accounting and invoices are retained according to applicable financial regulations.
11) Security
We apply reasonable administrative, technical, and organizational measures to protect personal data.
    However, no online service can guarantee absolute security.
12) Your Rights
➜ Depending on your jurisdiction, you may request access, correction, deletion, restriction, objection, or portability of your data.
➜ To request changes or exercise rights, contact:
info@asar-research.org
13) Children’s Privacy
Our services are not directed to children under 16 (or the minimum age required by local law). We do not knowingly collect data from children.
14) Photos / Recordings
The conference may include photos, video, or audio recordings for documentation and dissemination (website, reports, or communication materials),
    where announced. When required, we will provide notice and obtain consent.
15) Third-Party Links
Our website may include links or embedded tools from third parties. We are not responsible for their privacy practices.
    Please review their policies.
16) Changes to This Policy
We may update this policy from time to time. The “Last updated” date will reflect changes.
17) Contact

Title: ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
ASAR - Academic Society for Applied Research
A
ASAR
Independent Academic Initiative
Promoting Collaboration Between Research, Education & Industry
The Academic Society for Applied Research (ASAR) fosters global dialogue and drives innovation in the field of applied research.
Who We Are
A Global Academic Initiative
ASAR is an independent, academic-oriented initiative with global reach. We work to strengthen collaboration between researchers, educators, practitioners, and institutions involved in applied research across different scientific fields.
Our foundation is built on the belief that interdisciplinary cooperation is key to solving complex modern challenges. By bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and industrial application, we create value for society.
Our Mission
"To promote and support applied research by creating opportunities for scientific dissemination, academic dialogue, and cooperation between the academic sector, research institutions, and industry."
Key Objectives
Encourage development and dissemination of applied research.
Facilitate collaboration among researchers and institutions.
Organize conferences and academic meetings.
Support publication in recognized and indexed channels.
Our Pillars
What We Do
Conferences
ASAR promotes international scientific meetings that bring together researchers and professionals. We provide spaces for dialogue, knowledge exchange, and the dissemination of research results.
Publications
We collaborate with journals and editorial partners to support the publication of scientific work derived from our events. We aim to contribute to the visibility of quality research.
Research
Fostering collaboration between academics, universities, and organizations. We promote initiatives that stimulate scientific development, methodological strengthening, and innovation.

About us
The Congress on Information Technologies and Digital Innovation (CITDI 2026) is an event dedicated to advances in computing, artificial intelligence, immersive technologies and digital transformation, bringing together the wide spectrum of fields shaping today’s digital society.
CITDI 2026, organized by the Academic Society for Applied Research, builds upon previous initiatives that have connected researchers and professionals working on intelligent systems, data-driven technologies and emerging digital solutions.
The congress offers a unique opportunity for discussion and collaboration among scientists, engineers, educators, innovators and industry partners, allowing participants to explore applications, share insights and engage with technologies that define the future of digital innovation.
